headlineSummary: Anthropic drew the most positive discussion, Microsoft Copilot drew the most negative, and OpenAI was mildly negative; Anthropic’s praise outweighed a small openness complaint, while Copilot was criticized for hurting code quality [E1][E2][E4][E5][E6].

ranking.summary: Microsoft Copilot had clearly negative sentiment, with one comment expressing annoyance at repeated mentions and another saying its absence probably improved code quality [E5][E6].
OpenAI had mixed but slightly negative sentiment on limited evidence, with one comment describing Codex as something users might be locked into [E2].
Anthropic was strongly positive overall, with praise for Claude Code as essential to product work and a valuable, actionable writeup, offset slightly by one lock-in concern [E1][E2][E3][E4].

evidence.excerpt: If you are building a product today, the feature you are working on _is not done_ until Claude Code can use it. A skill and an MCP isn't a "nice to have", it is going to be as important as SEO and accessibility, with extremely similar work to do to enable it.

annotations.rationale: The comment says product work is not complete until Claude Code can use it and calls related support as important as SEO/accessibility, which is strong praise for Claude Code's usefulness and importance.

evidence.excerpt: Means you're not locked into Claude or Codex

annotations.rationale: Framing MCP support as avoiding being 'locked into Claude' treats Claude as a lock-in option, a mild negative toward Anthropic's ecosystem openness.
Framing MCP support as avoiding being 'locked into ... Codex' presents Codex as a lock-in option, a mild negative toward OpenAI's ecosystem openness.

evidence.excerpt: Beyond that, I'd just keep using Claude Code in the terminal.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly prefers continuing to use Claude Code, indicating favorable sentiment toward the product.

evidence.excerpt: it's a valuable piece of writing. - It's short and to the point - It's actionable in the short term (make sure the tasks per session aren't too difficult) and useful for researchers in the long term - It's informative on how these models work, informed by some of the best in the business

annotations.rationale: On an Anthropic Alignment post, the commenter strongly praises the writeup as valuable, actionable, and informed by 'some of the best in the business,' which is strong positive sentiment toward Anthropic's safety/alignment work.

evidence.excerpt: So help me God anyone mentions Copilot one more time...

annotations.rationale: The commenter expresses frustration at hearing about Copilot, a clear mild negative reaction to the product.

evidence.excerpt: Copilot being down probably increased code quality

annotations.rationale: Saying Copilot being unavailable 'probably increased code quality' is a direct, strongly negative judgment about Copilot's coding output quality.
